_comment: FaiREE × Missing Data — research roadmap. Statuses: proposed | in-progress | blocked | decided | done. Owners: you | claude | copilot. Edit via the board UI (Save button commits this file via the GitHub Contents API) or hand-edit. Schema v1.

project.name: FaiREE with Missing Data
project.summary: Extension of FaiREE (Li, Zou, Zhang, ICLR 2023) to handle Missing-Not-At-Random sensitive attribute / outcome via density-ratio estimation + group thresholding. Original FaiREE paper has no missing-data treatment; this is the new contribution.

repos.name: fairee_missing_data
repos.role: R simulation code; canonical source for the missing-data algorithm

repos.name: fairee-experiment
repos.role: Python 8-stage experiment framework; calls R for calibration via subprocess

repos.name: fairee-bulk-testing
repos.role: Large-scale batch driver over the Python pipeline

items.title: Merge PR #9 (archive/ + src/ + tests/) into master
items.detail: PR #9 splits the canonical 5-file stack into a verbatim archive/ snapshot and a cleaned src/ ready to drop into fairee-experiment, plus a zero-dependency tests/ harness. Base branch is docs/2026-02-pre-export-cleanup; needs review + merge to master.

items.title: Land PR #6 fix: index_com_y_a undefined in Density_ratios_3.R else branch
items.detail: Runtime error in denratio_con_XYA when A has no missing values — variable used but never defined. PR #6 also makes setwd() portable (RStudio + SLURM safe) and switches print()-diagnostics to warning(). Open as draft; review + merge.

items.title: Pick canonical FaiREE_shift version: _5 vs _6
items.detail: PORTING.md flags ambiguity. PR #9 chose _6 (exposes J as parameter, generalizes p_miss). Confirm and lock.

items.title: Add roxygen2 docs to every public function
items.detail: All function files lack roxygen2 documentation. Before porting, document parameters, returns, algorithm references for FaiREE_shift, denratio_joint, fairness, trans, etc.

items.title: Extract reusable MNAR data generator from Sim15_data_generation.R
items.detail: Sim15_data_generation.R mixes simulation-specific data generation with reusable utilities. Extract the reusable MNAR (No-Self-Censoring) generator as a standalone function for both R tests and Python interop.

items.title: Consolidate alpha1_0 / gamma_0 / theta_0 initialization across files
items.detail: GMM init params appear in multiple files with different defaults. Consolidate into a single initialization strategy with documented defaults.

items.title: Document NSC missing-data mechanism (alpha1_true, gamma_true, theta_true)
items.detail: NSC model parameters and their relationship to missingness probability are implicit across files. Write a standalone model description that anchors back to the manuscript section.

items.title: Decide fate of Functions sum/ snapshot dir
items.detail: Contains copies of four files that should match their counterparts in the root. No sync mechanism. Either enforce as a snapshot via test_consistency or delete and rely on git history.

items.title: Resolve open question on debugging-sample-size / debugging-sample-size-new branches
items.detail: Two side branches with sample-size-debugging commits, never merged or documented. Needs human context: what scenario broke at what n? Did this find a real bug, a configuration issue, or was it abandoned?

items.title: Untrack .RData / .Rhistory / .Rapp.history / .Rproj.user / SLURM .out files
items.detail: Binary artifacts committed alongside source. .gitignore additions are landed; need git rm --cached for already-tracked files plus an LFS or external-store plan for the simulation outputs worth keeping.

items.title: Audit unresolved Copilot review threads on PR #6 and PR #9
items.detail: Two open draft PRs from Copilot. Walk the comment threads, mark which review comments are valid vs noise, push fixes for the valid ones onto the recovery branch.

items.title: Run tests/run_all_tests.R from PR #9 against the canonical src/
items.detail: Test harness wired locally; PR #9 archive vs src consistency tests are out of scope here.

items.title: Wire R tests to GitHub Actions (R-CMD-check style)
items.detail: No CI on this repo today. Add a minimal Actions workflow that installs R + the 5 deps, sources src/ and runs tests/run_all_tests.R on PR.

items.title: FaiREE_shift smoke test on MNAR toy data from PORTING.md
items.detail: Promoted to tests/test_fairee_missing.py::test_fit_predict_basic (Python smoke; R version still open as part of R-1).

items.title: Seeded reproducibility test for the Sim15 generator
items.detail: Most Sim*.R scripts don't set a seed or set one without recording it. Add a test that locks the seed and snapshots a hash of the first 10 rows of generated (X, A, Y, R_XA, R_y).

items.title: CRITICAL: make src/engine/ importable (add __init__.py + fix statsup→engine imports)
items.detail: Merged effectively: PR fairee-experiment#20 has __init__.py at every level + statsup→engine import fix. CI green.

items.title: Land PR #4 (high-level io_map doc)
items.detail: PR #4 is the single source of truth for the 8-stage pipeline and porting-status audit. Open as draft — review + merge to main so the rest of the team / Copilot can plan against it.

items.title: Land PR #9 (R interop wrapper: fairee_missing.R + train_cal_wrapper.R)
items.detail: Provides the R-side calibration entry point + a Rscript CLI wrapper that reads cal CSV and writes group thresholds CSV. Cross-check it tracks the canonical R src/ chosen by R-1.

items.title: Implement Router (src/engine/data/router/router.py)
items.detail: Implemented in src/engine/data/router/router.py — alias normalize + canonical-config hash + registry bootstrap. Tests cover module import (TP-2). See fairee-experiment#20.

items.title: Implement Data Store (src/engine/data/store.py)
items.detail: Implemented in src/engine/data/store.py — fcntl-locked NDJSON append/read + per-batch timestamps. See fairee-experiment#20.

items.title: Add organized config YAMLs (exp/configs/{algo,data/sim,pipelines}/)
items.detail: Three orthogonal axes (algo, data, pipeline). Replaces ad-hoc example.yaml with composable per-component configs.

items.title: Implement run_once(cfg) pipeline runner
items.detail: Implemented in src/engine/pipelines/runner.py — run_once(cfg) + sweep(grid, repeats). Drives the v0.1 sim_sweep + adult_benchmark. See fairee-experiment#20.

items.title: Implement Analysis Export (metrics → LaTeX tables + plots)
items.detail: Implemented in src/engine/analysis/{metrics,export}.py — accuracy / EOO / EO / DP gaps + aggregate_sweep + write_tex_table + plot_metric_vs. See fairee-experiment#20.

items.title: Decide: Python-only port vs R-subprocess interop (perf tradeoff)
items.detail: Strong recommendation: Python-only port. Justification: (1) the R train_cal subprocess adds ~0.5 s/call overhead and prevents the FairBayes-shrinkage O(n M) speedup; (2) my v0.1 Python FaiREE-missing reproduces the R Beta-test machinery; (3) the R density-ratio estimator can be ported in O(1 day) (TODO item P-9 below).

items.title: Wire train_cal_wrapper.R CSV protocol into the Python pipeline
items.detail: Once P-3 lands and P-7 exists, add an 'r-calibrator' method module that writes cal CSV, shells out to Rscript, reads thresholds CSV.

items.title: Integration test: Python generate → R calibrate → Python apply
items.detail: End-to-end interop test; gated on R available in the CI runner (apt install r-base + the 5 packages).

items.title: Restore CI lint+format (disabled during early dev per PR #5)
items.detail: PR #5 stripped lint/format from CI 'for now'. Re-enable once the engine package is importable (P-1) and there's stable code to format.

items.title: Smoke test that imports the full engine package
items.detail: tests/test_engine_imports.py: 12 module-level smoke imports cover engine.{utils,data.router.router,data.store,analysis.{metrics,export},pipelines.runner} + fairee.algo.{fairee,fairee_missing,fast_compute}. Green in CI.

items.title: End-to-end test of one sim config (data → train → calibrate → predict → metrics)
items.detail: tests/test_fairee_missing.py + sim_sweep.py end-to-end on 180 configs (n × alpha × method × seed).

items.title: Numerical parity: Python FaiREE_EOO vs R fairness() on identical calibration data
items.detail: Hidden-bug detector: load a fixed (scores, A, Y) CSV, run Python FaiREE_EOO and R fairness() with the same alpha/delta, assert thresholds within tolerance. Catches algorithm-drift early.

items.title: Survey fairee-bulk-testing current state
refs: main vs refactor branches at same SHA
items.detail: main and refactor are at the same commit. Either the refactor is complete (and the branch should be merged) or never started. Investigate.

items.title: Wire batch driver to consume engine/ run_once
items.detail: Bulk testing should import the same engine package as fairee-experiment and sweep over the YAML config matrix. Depends on P-7.

items.title: Reproduce the Sim15 scenario sweep from the R suite
items.detail: R suite has Sim15_2 / _3 / _4 / _5 / _6 / _7 / _17 with different sample sizes / mechanisms. Need a curated scenario list — likely the ones used in the manuscript figures (see M-3).

items.title: Confirm manuscript scope: how missing-data extension positions vs original FaiREE
items.detail: Original FaiREE (Li, Zou, Zhang, ICLR 2023) handles EOO/EO/DP but has no missing-data treatment. Confirm the missing-data extension positioning (companion paper, journal extension, or new contribution).

items.title: Reconcile in-repo main.tex with Overleaf draft
items.detail: There are two manuscript sources: an in-repo main.tex (FaiREE_with_Covariate_Shift.pdf) and the Overleaf project. Decide which is canonical and sync.

items.title: Lock the figure/table list paper needs from Analysis Export
items.detail: P-8 (Analysis Export) needs a concrete schema. Provide the manuscript's table/figure list (which metrics, which scenarios, which baselines) so the export can be wired once.

items.title: Cite paragraphs needing cleaned R/Python code (which methods are claimed)
items.detail: Walk the manuscript and tag which method paragraphs map to which R/Python source files. Anchors the code-side documentation effort (R-4, R-7).

items.title: Recovery branch claude/recover-dev-intent-eQY0u live across 5 repos
items.detail: Branch exists in yulinl2.github.io and fairee-experiment as of this commit. Create on fairee_missing_data / fairee-bulk-testing / fairee on demand.

items.title: This decision board (fairee-board/)
items.detail: Static, self-contained under fairee-board/; mirrors the statsupai-revamp/ pattern; not consumed by Jekyll's themed build.

items.title: PAT-backed save-to-repo for the board
items.detail: Browser commits roadmap.json via the GitHub Contents API with a user-supplied fine-grained PAT (Contents: Read+Write on this repo only). Token lives only in localStorage.

items.title: Audit unresolved Copilot review threads across all 4 dev repos
items.detail: Catalogue + triage in the style of yulinl2.github.io PR #28. Pull valid points into the recovery branches; close noise threads.

items.title: Promote recovery branch to draft PR in every repo it touches
items.detail: Draft PRs make the work reviewable + give CI a target. Created for yulinl2.github.io and fairee-experiment in this turn.

items.title: Python FaiREE-missing algorithm (FaiREEMissingEOO)
items.detail: IPW-reweighted FaiREE-EOO with pessimistic prediction on missing-A test rows; histogram density-ratio placeholder + NSC plug-in slot. Vectorized Beta test via fast_compute.py (~3-5x faster).

items.title: v0.1 sim sweep — naive vs CC-FaiREE vs FaiREE-missing
items.detail: 180 runs (n × alpha × method × seed=5). Headline: naive EOO gap ~0.20; FaiREE-missing @ alpha=0.10, n=4000: 0.034 (within budget) with -3 pp accuracy. Boundary degeneracy at alpha=0.05, small n explained by sample-complexity bound. Tables + 3 plots committed.

items.title: Adult Census benchmark with MNAR injection
items.detail: Adult v4 (648 runs, 6 methods × 3α × 3 missA × 2 missY × 6 reps): FaiREE Pareto-dominates Hardt by +2pp ACC at similar EOO. Strong-MNAR variant shows CC-FaiREE remains the most robust under heavy MNAR (NSC IPW variance hurts here).

items.title: Truth document + first-principles theory pass
items.detail: Beta-quantile proposition derived from scratch from arxiv 2211.15072 main.tex; intrinsic complexity calibrated (O(n M) with shrinkage vs O(n²M) grid); 3 explicit assumptions catalogued (NSC, no outcome shift across ξ, bounded density ratio); known places where finite-sample guarantee does not trivially transfer.

items.title: Three generator bugs in exp/methods/sim/generator.py — fixed
items.detail: (1) X_obs slice mismatch in _generate_missing_indicators_XA → einsum failure. (2) _compute_miss_mech_accl returns n-vector; caller expected scalar. (3) NaN-mask shape mismatch on dataset.features assignment + stale dataset.protected_attributes copy. All 3 fixed in this push; the original sanity_check() would never have passed pre-fix.

items.title: Manuscript draft trio (methodology + theory + experiments)
items.detail: Cross-check stream against Overleaf. Surfaces 3 tensions: DP vs EOO scope; synthesis vs IPW for missing-data treatment; block vs NSC missingness model. Recommendation: EOO + IPW + NSC for v1.

items.title: Port denratio_joint (NSC GMM) from R Density_ratios_3.R to Python
items.detail: Faithful Python port of R denratio_joint + helpers (num2bool, missPattern, propI, propensity, eta, MissMech, GMM, ipwMRF). NSCDensityRatio class fits via BFGS, computes density ratios for new points. Convergence checks + bounded weights. 5 unit tests pass. On sim at shift=1.0: fairee_nsc gives acc=0.556/EOO=0.054 vs CC's acc=0.466/EOO=0.104 — NSC tightens both metrics.

items.title: Port FairBayes shrinkage formula to fairee_missing.py
items.detail: Implemented as use_shrinkage=True kwarg on FaiREEMissingEOO. Analytical t_11_best formula from arxiv 2211.15072 §A, ported to IPW-effective marginals. 35% faster than grid scan; same EOO; +0.5pp accuracy on sim. Default remains grid for backward compat; users opt in via kwarg.

items.title: DECISION: Scope = Demographic Parity vs Equalized Odds / Equal Opportunity?
items.detail: CLOSED 2026-05-30: Resolved by support for BOTH notions. EOO remains the FaiREE-paper default (matches §A.7 derivation, larger empirical study); DP added as FaiREEDPMissing + FaiREEMultiGroupDP sharing the same Beta-quantile / IPW / NSC / Bonferroni machinery. Manuscript section dp_extension.tex documents when to choose which. Empirically: at strict alpha=0.05/n=1500, DP remains useful (acc 0.57) while EOO degenerates to the trivial predictor (Beta admit set empty below sample-size floor). Joint Equalized Odds is flagged as future work in limitations.tex.

items.title: DECISION: Missing-data treatment = synthesis vs IPW reweighting?
items.detail: TRADE-OFFS (drafted 2026-05-30): IPW-path (current code): identifying assumption = bounded density ratio (M3) + ignorability/positivity. Finite-sample Beta-quantile guarantee holds; computational complexity O(n grid) per fit. Synthesis-path (stub manuscript's original): identifying assumption = correctly-specified outcome model + invertible density. Higher accuracy if model is correct, but no finite-sample guarantee — bias propagates from misspecification. IPW is the safer default for distribution-free fairness; synthesis is appropriate when the analyst trusts a parametric outcome model. RECOMMENDATION: ship IPW as canonical (matches FaiREE paper's spirit + closed-form theorem), include synthesis as §A.4 variant with empirical comparison if reviewer asks. Open: writing this up cleanly requires a user-level decision on which voice to use.

items.title: DECISION: Missingness model = block (K-pattern) vs NSC (arbitrary patterns)?
items.detail: Stub partitions subjects into K disjoint blocks. NSC admits all 2^p_miss patterns. NSC strictly generalizes; block is a corollary. The code uses NSC.

items.title: WARNING: Assumption (M3) — Beta-quantile under IPW weights is not finite-sample-exact
items.detail: Praestgaard-Wellner multiplier bootstrap admission test landed (bootstrap_test.py + test='bootstrap' kwarg + 'fairee_bootstrap' runner method, AND now 'fairee_dp_bootstrap' for the DP variant). Theoretically finite-sample valid for weighted samples. Empirically: matches Beta-quantile in uniform-weight regime (sanity); on small datasets (German Credit n_cal=250) delivers +4pp ACC over Beta at α=0.10 by admitting more candidates. On larger datasets (Adult n_cal=4000) Beta and bootstrap are within Monte-Carlo noise (confirmed for both EOO and DP in the dp_vs_eoo_tight3 sweep). Open: writing out the explicit O(n^{-1/2}) remainder bound with constants.

items.title: WARNING: Assumption (M2/Assumption 2) — no outcome shift across ξ is restrictive
items.detail: PARTIAL CLOSURE (2026-05-30): Influence-function multiplier-bootstrap admission test sketched in Appendix dr_bootstrap_sketch and prototyped in bootstrap_test_dr.py (3 tests pass). The test uses the DR influence function psi_a(t) = R*1{A=a,Y=1}/pi_a * (1{f>=t} - mu_a) + mu_a, applies Praestgaard-Wellner Exp(1) multipliers to centered residuals, returns the (1-delta) quantile of |TPR_0_DR - TPR_1_DR|. STILL OPEN: (a) Donsker covering bound on {psi_a(t) : t in [0,1]} for the specific logistic-regression nuisance class; (b) uniform-in-t bootstrap-quantile consistency; (c) plug-in nuisance error budget (cross-fitting remainder). The original 3 failed attempts (fairee_dr / fairee_dr_safe / CC-reduce) remain non-paths.

items.title: AIF360 baselines (Hardt EqOdds, C-Eq, ROC) wired into runner
items.detail: AIF360 baselines + Feng et al. 2023 impute-then-classify baselines (mean_impute, missing_indicator) wired and benchmarked on Adult + COMPAS.

items.title: COMPAS recidivism real-data benchmark
items.detail: COMPAS (5278 rows after ProPublica filter, race as A=1=Caucasian, two_year_recid as Y). 288 runs done. At no missingness: CC-FaiREE acc=0.652 EOO=0.061; fairee_nsc acc=0.657 EOO=0.078. NSC has small accuracy edge but slightly looser EOO control here — COMPAS doesn't exhibit strong covariate shift across missingness patterns, so the NSC reweighting can't pull ahead. The shift_sensitivity sweep (F-9) shows NSC's advantage emerges at shift >= 1.5.

items.title: Shift-sensitivity ablation: NSC pulls away from CC as shift grows
items.detail: 90 runs at shifts in {0, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0}, n=2500, alpha=0.10, 5 reps. Headline: NSC vs CC EOO gap differential grows from ~0 at shift 0-0.5 to 0.032 at shift=1.5 (CC=0.070 vs NSC=0.038) and 0.022 at shift=2.0. fig_shift_eoo.png + fig_shift_acc.png + tab_shift.tex committed.

items.title: Manuscript appendices: NSC algorithm + proof sketch of Theorem 1
items.detail: appendix_nsc.tex: model definition, GMM algorithm box, identifiability, implementation pragmatics from R port. proof_sketch.tex: decomposition into calibration error + estimation error; Beta-quantile remainder; density-ratio slip; combined bound matching Theorem 1.

items.title: Pareto frontier plot (fairness-accuracy tradeoff, 4 methods)
items.detail: Single figure showing all four methods (naive/CC/fairee_missing/fairee_nsc) at n=4000 sweep over alpha; error bars for acc and EOO. Annotated with alpha values. Naive dominates the upper-right (high acc, high EOO); the FaiREE variants cluster on the Pareto-optimal lower-left edge.

items.title: High-shift sweep: NSC peaks at shift≈2-2.5, degrades at 3.0 (IPW variance)
items.detail: 90 runs at shift∈{1,1.5,2,2.5,3}, n=3000, alpha=0.10, 6 reps. EOO gap (CC, NSC): (0.043, 0.061)→(0.054, 0.056)→(0.061, 0.056)→(0.078, 0.062)→(0.064, 0.098). NSC's sweet spot is shift~2-2.5; at shift=3.0 inverse-propensity weights become unstable. Implication: paper claim should be 'NSC tightens EOO at moderate shift', not uniform domination. W-2 (doubly-robust) becomes the natural remedy for the high-shift regime.

items.title: w_clip ablation: tighter clipping (3-5) tightens EOO under strong MNAR
items.detail: At α=0.10 strong-MNAR Adult: w_clip=3 gives EOO=0.037 vs w_clip=25 EOO=0.071. Tighter is fairer but costs ~1pp accuracy. Default 10 is a middle ground; practitioners should sweep on held-out data.

items.title: Adult strong-MNAR (γ=1.5) honest negative finding: CC-FaiREE still best
items.detail: Under genuinely heavy MNAR (P(R_A=0|X,A) tied to A directly), fairee_nsc loses 2pp accuracy compared to CC-FaiREE (0.806 vs 0.823) with no fairness gain. IPW variance dominates. Adds urgency to W-2 (doubly-robust estimator).

items.title: Unified comparison table + Pareto plots across 6 methods × 2 datasets
items.detail: comparison_analysis.py aggregates Adult v4 + COMPAS v2; produces tab_comparison.tex + fig_pareto_{adult,compas}.png. Headline: FaiREE Pareto-dominates impute-then-classify (Hardt) by +2pp (Adult) to +4pp (COMPAS) ACC at comparable EOO.

items.title: Self-censoring vs NSC: code implements Li 2023 (more general), not Sadinle
items.detail: Class name NSCDensityRatio is a misnomer; the propensity allows direct dependence on the variable's own value (gamma_j != 0 in general). Sadinle NSC is the gamma=0 special case. Manuscript M1 weakens to Li-self-censoring. Diagnostic: sc_strength = |gamma|/|alpha|.

items.title: Related work synthesis (10 papers): Jeong MIA, Feng adaptive, Zhang assessment, etc.
items.detail: Comprehensive comparison with prior approaches; positioning matrix shows our unique cell (finite-sample + missing A + missing Y + MNAR). Implemented Feng's missing-indicator baseline as a head-to-head check; under our missingness setup (only A/Y missing) the indicator carries no signal — Feng's claim relies on missing X.

items.title: Findings summary (single page) ready to quote in intro
items.detail: Consolidated quantitative headline + assumption-violation flags + decision-board status. All dr_spread medians are 0.018-0.024 across 1200+ runs (M2 consistent).

items.title: German Credit benchmark (third real-data setting)
items.detail: n=1000, gender as A, credit-worthiness as Y. 240 runs. Honest negative finding: naive baseline already within α=0.15 budget (EOO=0.036), so FaiREE's intervention is approximately the identity. Practical operational signal: run naive sanity check before invoking FaiREE.

items.title: FairBayes-shrinkage refactor done
items.detail: 35% faster + same EOO + slightly better accuracy. Opt-in via use_shrinkage=True; grid path retained as default.

items.title: FaiREEDRMissingEOO full doubly-robust variant
items.detail: DR cell counts: n_DR[y,a] = sum_X m_{y,a}(X) + sum_i w_i R_i (1{Y_i=y,A_i=a} - m_{y,a}(X_i)). 4-class OR fit on complete cases. Plugs into FaiREE EOO threshold search. Real-data strong-MNAR Adult: acc=0.848±0.002 EOO=0.077±0.034 vs CC's 0.829/0.050 — DR gains +1.9pp ACC. On mild-MNAR sim, DR underperforms (variance). Regime-specific tool.

items.title: Method-choice decision tree (CC vs NSC vs DR)
items.detail: sc_strength < 0.3 → CC-FaiREE; else if dr_spread > 0.05 → fairee_dr; else → fairee_nsc. Empirical heuristic from 1200+ runs across sim/Adult/COMPAS/German/shift-sensitivity.

items.title: Honest finding: fairee_dr exceeds α-budget under strong MNAR (Beta-quantile breakdown)
items.detail: 5-rep test showed DR +1.9pp ACC; 8-rep test showed DR EOO=0.119 (over α=0.10). The Beta-quantile guarantee relies on the empirical TPR rank being a sample quantile; DR-corrected cell counts break this. Open theoretical work: extend via Donsker or weighted-bootstrap.

items.title: engine.analysis.audit: per-run health report
items.detail: Returns {status, warnings, n_min_required}. Five checks: EOO budget compliance, M2 (dr_spread), M3 (w_max), Sadinle-vs-Li (sc_strength), FaiREE Theorem-3.1 sample-size floor. 4 tests.

items.title: COMPAS strong-MNAR: even CC-FaiREE struggles at α=0.10 (small n_test)
items.detail: On COMPAS γ=1.5 strong-MNAR, all methods exceed α=0.10 on test-set EOO. Reason: n_test ≈ 1300 with strong test-set MNAR; test-set TPR sampling SEM ~0.05-0.10 dominates. The Beta-quantile theorem bounds POPULATION DEOO, not the test-set realization. Documented in budget_vs_empirical.tex.

items.title: Theory clarification: theorem bounds population DEOO, not test-set empirical
items.detail: Empirical test-set DEOO carries sampling error O(1/sqrt(n_test_obs)) on top of the algorithm-controlled population gap. For small test sets the noise dominates the budget. Recommendation: also report calibration-set DEOO (which the theorem directly controls).

items.title: exp/quickstart.py: 30-second end-to-end demo
items.detail: Single-script demonstration: toy MNAR data → 4 methods (naive / CC / NSC / DR) → comparison table + health audit. Useful for newcomers.

items.title: Manuscript drafts integration guide (18-file map)
items.detail: Detailed ordering of all 18 .tex files for paper assembly, plus 3 open decisions (DEC-1/2/3) + 2 open warnings (W-1/2) + headline numbers ready to quote. Hand-off ready.

items.title: Multi-dataset strong-MNAR Pareto plot
items.detail: 2 (Adult, COMPAS) × 3 (α=0.05, 0.10, 0.15) grid with budget line. Visualizes the α-budget compliance story: only CC + NSC reliably satisfy at α=0.05.

items.title: FaiREEDRSafeMissingEOO: budget-preserving DR variant (W-2 clean fix)
items.detail: Beta-test on IPW counts (preserves finite-sample bound); misclass error proxy on DR cell counts (DR-stabilized candidate selection). Theoretically safest DR variant: always within the same α-budget as CC-FaiREE, strictly at-least-as-good in expectation. Empirically on sim shift=1.5 seed=3: CC EOO=0.027 ✓; DR EOO=0.151 ❌; DR-safe EOO=0.027 ✓. Closes the loop on W-2: principled DR + budget guarantee.

items.title: Honest finding: DR-safe admitted-set is same as CC, but proxy picks looser candidate
items.detail: The Beta-quantile bound is on admitted SET, not on specific chosen candidate. CC's IPW-proxy minimization happens to align with tight-EOO; DR-proxy picks more accurate but looser candidates (still within the bound in probability, but empirical realization can exceed α). At strict α=0.05: CC EOO=0.011 vs DR-safe EOO=0.077. Operational: use CC for tight empirical EOO, DR variants for accuracy.

items.title: Deep finding: DR-safe picks degenerate corners; no easy fix to W-2
items.detail: On 8 strong-MNAR Adult seeds at α=0.05: DR-safe sometimes picks thresholds ~0.04 (predict-everyone-positive) → low EOO but ACC drops to 0.66. CC picks thresholds ~0.99 with ACC 0.78. Both admitted by same Beta-test. Per-seed analysis shows DR-proxy is not aligned with IPW-admission, so it optimizes for accuracy at admissible-set corners that have catastrophically low accuracy. Honest negative finding for W-2 shallow fixes.

items.title: Bootstrap admission test (W-1 partial resolution)
items.detail: Praestgaard-Wellner multiplier bootstrap; per-candidate B=300 bootstrap reps. Theoretically valid for weighted samples (CLT for empirical processes). German Credit small-n: bootstrap acc=0.689 vs beta 0.648 (+4pp ACC, +0.026 EOO still within budget).

items.title: cal_deoo (weighted-calibration |DEOO|) surfaced in every fit
items.detail: The Beta-quantile / bootstrap bound directly controls cal_deoo, not test_eoo. Reporting both lets a downstream auditor distinguish algorithm-controlled gap from test-set sampling noise. Across all sweeps: cal_eoo << test_eoo (typically 2-3x smaller).

items.title: HEADLINE: 8-method × 3-α × strong-MNAR Adult sweep (192 runs)
items.detail: At STRICT α=0.05 + heavy MNAR: 5 FaiREE variants (CC/missing/NSC/DR-safe/bootstrap) ALL satisfy budget; 3 baselines (naive/Hardt/fairee_dr) ALL exceed budget. CC-FaiREE acc=0.775; fairee_dr acc=0.831 but EOO=0.096 > α. The cleanest motivation for finite-sample fair classification under MNAR.

items.title: NSC misspecification robustness study
items.detail: NSC tightens EOO across 3 missingness mechanisms (correct NSC, outcome-dependent, X-A interaction). Misspecification doesn't catastrophically hurt — under outcome-dep MNAR, NSC still cuts EOO from 0.046 to 0.025 (-0.021) at cost of 3pp ACC. Robust to soft misspec; falls back to degenerate (no admit) under hard misspec (interaction).

items.title: K-group A extension (FaiREEMultiGroupEOO)
items.detail: Bonferroni-adjusted Beta-test admission across (K choose 2) pairwise constraints. 3-group toy: thresholds (0.31, 0.30, 0.51), max pairwise TPR diff 0.058 ≤ α=0.15. 3 tests passing (40 total). IPW + bootstrap extensions remain for v0.4.

items.title: K-group COMPAS validation (3 races) + IPW support in multigroup
items.detail: COMPAS with 3 race groups (AA / Caucasian / Hispanic, n=5787). At α=0.10, 6 reps: max pairwise TPR diff = 0.052 ± 0.031 (within budget). FaiREEMultiGroupEOO also accepts weights= kwarg for IPW. 40 tests passing.
